description: LTX-2.3 22B structural control via the 'LTX-2.3-22b-IC-LoRA-Union-Control' IC-LoRA — canny / depth / pose conditioning from a reference video. You give it a control/reference clip; on Spark it extracts canny edges (CannyEdgePreprocessor), depth (VideoDepthAnything), and pose (DWPreprocessor), and the union IC-LoRA steers generation to follow that structure. TIER-2 / IC-LoRA: rides a model patch + a guide-conditioning path, so it's its own preset (not a stackable catalog LoRA). Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_ICLoRA_Union_Control_Distilled'.

about.what: Makes a new video that copies the shape and movement of a reference clip — its outlines (canny), its depth, or the people's body poses — so the result moves like the reference but looks like whatever your prompt describes.
about.inputs: A reference video (its motion and shape are traced for you) and a written description of the new look.
about.key_knobs: Prompt — describes the new scene (NOT the structure; that comes from the reference). The three structure guides — outlines, depth, pose — are all on by default; mute the ones you don't want.

_TEMPLATE: READY-TO-RUN. The TWO shared LTX-2.3 base weights (dev checkpoint, gemma text encoder) are pinned to real URLs — same two as ltx2_generate / ltx_hdr (verified). The distill LoRA is now PINNED to the cond-safe rank-72 variant (not the official rank-384, which dampens the canny/depth/pose conditioning this preset relies on) and the Union-Control IC-LoRA URL is REAL/verified. RUN --convert-only FIRST: it skips the weight pull and surfaces any missing node classes, which is how you confirm the preprocessor node_packs below are right.
_CONTROL: The graph wires ALL THREE preprocessors (canny 4991, pose 4986 DWPreprocessor, depth 5060/5061 VideoDepthAnything) into the union guide. Which one(s) drive the output depends on the graph's guide wiring; to favor one control type, mute the others or tune the guide via --set after pulling the converted workflow_api.json. Preprocessor MODELS (yolox_l.onnx + dw-ll_ucoco for pose, video_depth_anything_vits.pth for depth) are normally auto-downloaded by their node packs on first run; if a node errors 'model not found', add it to the models list.
_HOWITRUNS: No-build path: public base image, comfy_run.py clones node_packs (incl. the controlnet-aux + depth-anything preprocessors), downloads weights, converts UI->API, applies patches, optionally splices a Tier-1 --lora stack onto the checkpoint (anchor 3940), and renders. Heaviest cold start of the presets (base weights + preprocessor models).
_CONSTRAINTS: IC-LoRA guide constraints (verified by smoke render): (1) output width/height must give an EVEN latent — i.e. each be divisible by 64 (a non-÷64 dim like height 288 -> latent 9 fails 'latent spatial size must be divisible by 2'); 512x512 works. (2) output frame count (length) must be >= the control clip's frame count, else 'conditioning frames exceed the length of the latent sequence' (a 48-frame control clip needs length >= 49, i.e. the next 8n+1). The init image (node 2004 -> LTXVImgToVideoConditionOnly 3159) is BYPASSED by default (node 5019 = true) — structural control comes from the reference VIDEO; the init image only sets the first frame when bypass is turned off.
_INITIMAGE: Node 2004 (LoadImage) is the optional init/first-frame image, bypassed by default. It still needs a loadable file; a small placeholder is bundled (static_inputs) so the preset runs with just the control video. To actually use an init frame, pass your own image via --input plus --set 2004.inputs.image=<basename> and --set 5019.inputs.value=false.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Control — canny / depth / pose from a reference clip (IC-LoRA)

primary_input.label: Control reference video
primary_input.tooltip: The clip whose structure (edges/depth/pose) the output should follow. Preprocessing happens on Spark. Output resolution is set by the Width/Height/Frames knobs below (default 960x544).

prompt.default: A knight in ornate plate armor walks through a misty forest at dawn, shafts of warm light cutting through the trees. Slow tracking shot, shallow depth of field, the soft crunch of footsteps on leaves.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: Describe the OUTPUT scene you want — the structure (edges/depth/pose) is taken from the reference clip, NOT the prompt. Same LTX-2.3 single-paragraph style as generation (shot, lighting, action, camera, audio). (src: Union-Control example workflow + LTX-2.3 prompt guide)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Lightricks' shipped LTX-2 negative (same one used across their example workflows).

ui.label: Width
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 up to 4K/UHD (3840x2160). For THIS IC-LoRA preset keep width divisible by 64 (the guide needs an even latent; a non-÷64 size errors 'latent must be divisible by 2'). Best matched to the reference clip's aspect. 4K is heavy and only for clips <=10s. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Height
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 up to 4K/UHD. Keep divisible by 64 for this IC-LoRA preset (even latent). Default 576 (was 544, which gives an odd latent and fails the guide). Match the reference clip's aspect. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 121, 161). Should match the reference clip's frame count for clean control. LTX-2.3 max ~10s @50fps or ~20s @25fps. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)
